optimization/fitting:
* **p > N: No**

  The paper does not provide explicit details on the parameter count (p) of the models used or the training set size (N). However, the study by Reggente et al. (2018) mentioned in the text leveraged machine learning to assess functional connectivity patterns. Typically, machine learning models used in neuroimaging studies, such as those involving functional connectivity, do not have an excessively high parameter count relative to the number of training samples. Therefore, it is reasonable to infer that the parameter count is not larger than the training set size.

* **Over-fitting Evidence: Not enough information is available**

  The paper does not provide specific evidence or methods used to rule out over-fitting in the machine learning models employed. Therefore, it is not possible to determine what specific techniques were used to prevent over-fitting.

evaluation/confidence:
* Statistical Confidence:
  * CIs: No
  * p-values: Yes - "Significant correlation between YBOCS score changes and DAT binding ratio in rt basal ganglia" (Kim et al., 2007), "Pos correlation between duration of illness and SERT" (Zitterl et al., 2007), "Neg correlation between SERT availability and YBOCS severity" (Zitterl et al., 2007), "Baseline rCBF in b/l OFC correlated with improvement in YBOCS" (Yamanishi et al., 2009), "Pre-trt right PCC rCBF values pos correlated with reduction in YBOCS obsession scores" (Karadağ et al., 2013), "Pre-trt left thalamus and ACC uptake values neg correlated with reduction in YBOCS scores" (Karadağ et al., 2013), "Change in YBOCS neg correlated with pre-trt rCBF in left frontal lobe, left cingulate and b/l thalamus" (Wen et al., 2013), "Change in YBOCS neg correlated with the pre-trt rCBF in lt and rt frontal lobe" (Wen et al., 2013), "Neg correlation between PFC GBC and YBOCS severity" (Anticevic et al., 2014), "Pos correlation between GBC of putamen and cerebellum and YBOCS" (Anticevic et al., 2014), "Pos correlation between amygdala activation and severity of aggression/checking and sexual/religious symptoms" (Via et al., 2014), "Lower dmPFC self-system connectivity and connectivity of dmPFC with striatal and cingulate areas pos correlate with symptom severity" (Beucke et al., 2014), "Right insular intrinsic activity significantly correlated with total YBOCS severity" (Zhu et al., 2016).
